name: Sao Paulo state (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the night, recalling the bandeirantes who marched by night
the blood shed for the homeland; appears on the canton
the riches of the state, represented by the four golden stars on the canton
appears on the map of Brazil within the canton

description: Thirteen alternating horizontal stripes of black and white, with a red canton in the upper hoist charged with four yellow five-pointed stars surrounding a white disc bearing a blue map of Brazil.
history: Designed by the poet Julio Ribeiro and originally proposed in 1888 as a possible national flag for the Republic; rejected at federal level but later adopted as the flag of the State of Sao Paulo. Confirmed by State Law No. 145 of 3 September 1948.

features.symbolism: Black and white were the colours of the bandeirantes, the 17th-century Sao Paulo expeditions that explored the interior of Brazil.
Red canton with four yellow stars and a central white disc charged with a blue map of Brazil; recalls the standard of the bandeirantes

features.role: the four corners of the state
white roundel bearing a map of Brazil in blue

features.symbolism: Sao Paulo's role as a driver and protagonist of the Brazilian nation
